name: Trim offer complexity
description: Identify the menu or service elements creating disproportionate operational instability and simplify them until the current system can execute reliably again.
source_ref: core-v2: complexity should be reduced when the promise outruns the operation's current strength
entry_conditions: A subset of menu or service elements creates repeated instability, remakes, or bottlenecks.
Leadership is willing to remove, pause, simplify, or resequence parts of the offer.
contraindications: Do not trim complexity without linking decisions to observed execution pain and value impact.
Do not keep everything sacred when the operation is clearly unable to carry it.
proof_of_completion: A complexity review exists showing which offer elements create disproportionate drag.
At least one menu or service element has been simplified, paused, or resequenced.
The venue can explain why the trimmed complexity should improve execution reliability.
